description_long: This object is a vintage mechanical typewriter, featuring a robust metal body with a wide carriage and a full set of round, raised keys. The front section displays a densely packed keyboard with metallic key rims and dark keycaps, while the central area houses a prominent type basket and ribbon spools. The carriage extends significantly to both sides, equipped with knobs and levers for manual paper adjustment. The surface is predominantly black with metallic highlights, and the construction appears heavy and durable, typical of early 20th-century office equipment. The typewriter's design includes visible mechanical linkages and a paper feed mechanism at the rear. Its overall appearance suggests it was built for intensive, long-term use in an office or administrative urban setting.
description: A vintage black metal typewriter with a wide carriage, round keys, and visible mechanical components, built for durability and heavy use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full keyboard, type basket, and ribbon spools.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the carriage knob, side profile, and part of the key array.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the length of the carriage and paper feed mechanism.
description_view_3: Right side view with carriage lever, side profile, and partial keyboard visibility.

license_info.creator_display_name: Museum of Engineering and Technology, Krakow
